---
title: "aa_per_timepoint_fisher — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [8, 28, 22, 20, 22]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![3x3 grid of BWM-vs-control amino-acid volcano panels: rows are timepoints (Day 0 / Day 5 / Day 10) top to bottom and columns are ribosome sites (A-site / P-site / E-site) left to right. Each panel plots -log10(FDR) on the y-axis against log2(odds ratio) on the x-axis (x range -0.50 to +0.50); each glyph is the amino-acid one-letter code, coloured by physicochemical class (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 cells and circles mark non-significant cells; the dotted vertical line is log2(OR) = 0, the dashed vertical lines mark +/-0.50, and the dashed horizontal line marks FDR = 0.05.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is one amino acid at one (timepoint, site) BH-FDR family of 20; a glyph to the right of zero is BWM-enriched relative to control at that site and timepoint, to the left is BWM-depleted, and higher up the panel means a smaller FDR.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `aa_per_timepoint_fisher.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `per_timepoint_fisher`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `codon_per_timepoint_fisher.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), BWM vs control 2x2 contingency per (timepoint, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (timepoint, site, amino acid) 2x2 BWM-vs-control contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact; the 2 biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) are summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 9 (timepoint, site) families of 20 amino acids (180 tests total); effect column is `log2_OR` = log2 of the odds ratio (positive = BWM-enriched, negative = BWM-depleted).

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed before Fisher's exact; the source script literally prints a pseudoreplication warning. Anti-conservative p-values.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (1e5-1e6); Fisher's exact returns vanishing or exactly-zero p-values for tiny absolute deviations. The odds ratio is the primary effect column, not p.
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site)** — BH-FDR is computed within each (timepoint, site) family of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) tests. `p_adj` means "corrected within this slice", not across the whole CSV.
- **imbalanced-N** — BWM vs control totals differ per timepoint (global occupancy looks only mildly imbalanced, ~1.4x at day_0, milder than the stall-sites set's ~4.5x); Fisher handles imbalance fine but downstream interpretation should not over-read odds ratios when one cell is small.

---

## Headline

180 tests (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 20 amino acids). 159 clear FDR<0.05 (88.3%) and 164 clear FDR<0.10; the minimum adjusted p underflows to 0.0 (4 cells) and 106 of 180 cells sit at p_adj<1e-10. At whole-transcriptome pooled N (BWM/control totals 1.3M-3.4M per timepoint) FDR significance is near-universal, so p magnitude carries essentially no information here; the informative axis is effect size, and effect sizes are small — the largest single-cell `|log2_OR|` in the file is day_5 A:P +0.3300 (about a 1.26x odds ratio). Per-(timepoint, site) n_sig at FDR<0.05 is uniformly high, ranging only from 15/20 (day_10 A) to 20/20 (day_0 A and day_5 E), with no day_0-dominated power gradient — totals are near-balanced (ctrl:BWM 1.59 / 1.20 / 0.81 across day_0/day_5/day_10).

Largest-magnitude single-cell BWM-vs-control divergences, by `|log2_OR|`, mixing directions: day_5 A:P +0.3300, day_5 E:Y +0.2272, day_5 E:K -0.2260, day_10 A:W -0.2209, day_5 P:W +0.2206, day_10 A:N +0.2048, day_0 E:P +0.1974, day_5 A:E -0.1905 / day_0 A:D -0.1905. Largest-magnitude cells in the cross-timepoint concordant set (same OR direction at all three timepoints; reported at equal billing with the divergences): enriched P:W +0.122 (mean log2OR), E:F +0.110, E:C +0.107, A:Q +0.086, P:Q +0.085; depleted E:K -0.126, A:E -0.084, E:E -0.082, P:E -0.081, E:N -0.066. Of the 60 (site, amino acid) cells, 18 are direction-concordant across all three timepoints (12 enriched, 6 depleted) and 42 show at least one sign change; 36 of those 42 register the change at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows, but at this N a "significant flip" is N-driven (see `flip-sig-large-N-artifact`), not evidence of 36 biological reversals.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (the log2 of the odds ratio): positive means BWM-enriched relative to control at that (timepoint, site, amino acid), negative means BWM-depleted. `p_adj` is the two-sided Fisher's exact p after BH correction within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 amino acids. Because the pooled per-timepoint totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts, almost every cell clears FDR<0.05 and p magnitude is uninformative; the `large-N` flag marks every row whose `p_adj` is below 1e-10 (a blank flag means FDR-significant but `p_adj` at or above 1e-10). Rank rows by `|log2_OR|`, not by p. Each (timepoint, direction) table merges all three ribosome sites; rows are grouped by `site` in A -> P -> E order and within each site show the up-to-5 amino acids with the largest `|log2_OR|` (every row shown clears FDR<0.05).

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site | amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | W (tryptophan) | +0.4526 | 0.00e+00 | large-N |
| A | C (cysteine) | +0.1858 | 3.42e-75 | large-N |
| A | L (leucine) | +0.1580 | 1.48e-251 | large-N |
| A | P (proline) | +0.1047 | 9.16e-69 | large-N |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | +0.0881 | 2.13e-43 | large-N |
| P | G (glycine) | +0.1439 | 2.25e-199 | large-N |
| P | D (aspartate) | +0.1283 | 6.44e-160 | large-N |
| P | F (phenylalanine) | +0.0728 | 2.34e-31 | large-N |
| P | R (arginine) | +0.0480 | 7.81e-18 | large-N |
| P | A (alanine) | +0.0367 | 1.92e-11 | large-N |
| E | P (proline) | +0.1974 | 1.56e-267 | large-N |
| E | C (cysteine) | +0.0975 | 2.63e-16 | large-N |
| E | F (phenylalanine) | +0.0739 | 5.22e-28 | large-N |
| E | L (leucine) | +0.0660 | 2.68e-41 | large-N |
| E | A (alanine) | +0.0378 | 4.43e-12 | large-N |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_0 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site | amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | D (aspartate) | -0.1905 | 2.21e-289 | large-N |
| A | I (isoleucine) | -0.1772 | 4.09e-226 | large-N |
| A | N (asparagine) | -0.1743 | 2.43e-166 | large-N |
| A | V (valine) | -0.1246 | 1.86e-120 | large-N |
| A | M (methionine) | -0.0622 | 9.07e-13 | large-N |
| P | M (methionine) | -0.1279 | 1.23e-45 | large-N |
| P | E (glutamate) | -0.1163 | 2.33e-120 | large-N |
| P | K (lysine) | -0.0812 | 2.26e-66 | large-N |
| P | Y (tyrosine) | -0.0743 | 4.07e-27 | large-N |
| P | L (leucine) | -0.0694 | 9.82e-43 | large-N |
| E | H (histidine) | -0.1132 | 2.57e-37 | large-N |
| E | E (glutamate) | -0.0745 | 1.87e-54 | large-N |
| E | N (asparagine) | -0.0495 | 9.51e-18 | large-N |
| E | M (methionine) | -0.0476 | 2.57e-08 |  |
| E | K (lysine) | -0.0450 | 3.65e-26 | large-N |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site | amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | P (proline) | +0.3300 | 0.00e+00 | large-N |
| A | A (alanine) | +0.1925 | 5.39e-219 | large-N |
| A | L (leucine) | +0.1764 | 8.91e-249 | large-N |
| A | W (tryptophan) | +0.1688 | 1.41e-36 | large-N |
| A | R (arginine) | +0.0720 | 3.11e-34 | large-N |
| P | W (tryptophan) | +0.2206 | 2.98e-42 | large-N |
| P | Q (glutamine) | +0.2040 | 6.68e-157 | large-N |
| P | H (histidine) | +0.1711 | 5.96e-78 | large-N |
| P | P (proline) | +0.1111 | 5.61e-57 | large-N |
| P | K (lysine) | +0.0994 | 4.79e-79 | large-N |
| E | Y (tyrosine) | +0.2272 | 1.81e-166 | large-N |
| E | P (proline) | +0.2109 | 1.04e-209 | large-N |
| E | F (phenylalanine) | +0.2074 | 2.82e-179 | large-N |
| E | A (alanine) | +0.1845 | 9.43e-200 | large-N |
| E | T (threonine) | +0.1497 | 1.42e-122 | large-N |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_5 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site | amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | E (glutamate) | -0.1905 | 0.00e+00 | large-N |
| A | K (lysine) | -0.1748 | 8.85e-263 | large-N |
| A | I (isoleucine) | -0.1612 | 3.76e-179 | large-N |
| A | N (asparagine) | -0.1499 | 6.36e-122 | large-N |
| A | M (methionine) | -0.1062 | 2.11e-33 | large-N |
| P | V (valine) | -0.1283 | 3.45e-120 | large-N |
| P | E (glutamate) | -0.1157 | 9.84e-105 | large-N |
| P | F (phenylalanine) | -0.0997 | 7.09e-53 | large-N |
| P | D (aspartate) | -0.0876 | 1.19e-65 | large-N |
| P | I (isoleucine) | -0.0789 | 3.09e-44 | large-N |
| E | K (lysine) | -0.2260 | 0.00e+00 | large-N |
| E | M (methionine) | -0.1999 | 8.49e-116 | large-N |
| E | W (tryptophan) | -0.1760 | 1.57e-36 | large-N |
| E | G (glycine) | -0.1333 | 2.55e-113 | large-N |
| E | E (glutamate) | -0.1237 | 1.09e-132 | large-N |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_10 — Enriched (BWM > control)

| site | amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | N (asparagine) | +0.2048 | 2.94e-128 | large-N |
| A | Q (glutamine) | +0.1352 | 2.25e-48 | large-N |
| A | I (isoleucine) | +0.0491 | 3.57e-11 | large-N |
| A | M (methionine) | +0.0489 | 2.20e-05 |  |
| A | K (lysine) | +0.0474 | 1.84e-12 | large-N |
| P | W (tryptophan) | +0.1428 | 2.29e-12 | large-N |
| P | L (leucine) | +0.1272 | 3.08e-75 | large-N |
| P | V (valine) | +0.0863 | 1.10e-33 | large-N |
| P | S (serine) | +0.0579 | 9.51e-15 | large-N |
| P | M (methionine) | +0.0417 | 7.84e-04 |  |
| E | C (cysteine) | +0.1557 | 8.59e-23 | large-N |
| E | W (tryptophan) | +0.1170 | 6.59e-11 | large-N |
| E | S (serine) | +0.0888 | 6.93e-35 | large-N |
| E | L (leucine) | +0.0827 | 1.58e-36 | large-N |
| E | F (phenylalanine) | +0.0474 | 4.13e-07 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### day_10 — Depleted (BWM < control)

| site | amino acid | effect (`log2_OR`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | W (tryptophan) | -0.2209 | 1.51e-43 | large-N |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | -0.1909 | 8.70e-99 | large-N |
| A | P (proline) | -0.1234 | 8.85e-48 | large-N |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.0984 | 1.30e-40 | large-N |
| A | L (leucine) | -0.0676 | 5.94e-25 | large-N |
| P | Y (tyrosine) | -0.1160 | 1.10e-33 | large-N |
| P | N (asparagine) | -0.0849 | 1.28e-29 | large-N |
| P | C (cysteine) | -0.0519 | 1.92e-04 |  |
| P | R (arginine) | -0.0416 | 9.37e-08 |  |
| P | P (proline) | -0.0385 | 9.81e-06 |  |
| E | K (lysine) | -0.1084 | 3.29e-74 | large-N |
| E | P (proline) | -0.1042 | 1.91e-35 | large-N |
| E | E (glutamate) | -0.0481 | 1.24e-13 | large-N |
| E | N (asparagine) | -0.0403 | 4.13e-07 |  |
| E | I (isoleucine) | -0.0254 | 1.10e-03 |  |

### Cross-timepoint summary

These tables describe, per (site, amino acid) cell, how the BWM-vs-control direction behaves across the three timepoints. They are per-cell descriptive summaries, not a trajectory claim. `#sig` is the number of the 3 timepoints reaching `p_adj` < 0.05; in the flip table `*` marks each per-timepoint `log2_OR` at `p_adj` < 0.05; the per-timepoint triple is in chronological order (day_0, day_5, day_10).

#### Direction concordance (18 of 60 cells: same direction at all 3 timepoints)

12 enriched, 6 depleted. Each sub-table is sorted by `#sig` descending, then `|mean log2_OR|` descending.

##### Concordance — BWM-enriched (mean log2_OR > 0)

| cell | mean log2_OR | per-tp log2_OR | #sig | min p_adj | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| E:F | +0.110 | +0.07, +0.21, +0.05 | 3/3 | 2.82e-179 | large-N |
| E:C | +0.107 | +0.10, +0.07, +0.16 | 3/3 | 8.59e-23 | large-N |
| A:Q | +0.086 | +0.06, +0.06, +0.14 | 3/3 | 2.25e-48 | large-N |
| P:Q | +0.085 | +0.02, +0.20, +0.03 | 3/3 | 6.68e-157 | large-N |
| E:L | +0.076 | +0.07, +0.08, +0.08 | 3/3 | 3.41e-51 | large-N |
| E:V | +0.042 | +0.04, +0.08, +0.01 | 3/3 | 8.76e-41 | large-N |
| A:S | +0.042 | +0.06, +0.03, +0.04 | 3/3 | 6.25e-26 | large-N |
| P:W | +0.122 | +0.00, +0.22, +0.14 | 2/3 | 2.98e-42 | large-N |
| E:A | +0.079 | +0.04, +0.18, +0.01 | 2/3 | 9.43e-200 | large-N |
| A:R | +0.048 | +0.07, +0.07, +0.00 | 2/3 | 1.42e-37 | large-N |
| P:S | +0.028 | +0.02, +0.01, +0.06 | 2/3 | 9.51e-15 | large-N |
| A:G | +0.022 | +0.04, +0.01, +0.01 | 2/3 | 7.05e-17 | large-N |

: {tbl-colwidths="[10, 16, 30, 10, 16, 18]"}

##### Concordance — BWM-depleted (mean log2_OR < 0)

| cell | mean log2_OR | per-tp log2_OR | #sig | min p_adj | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| E:K | -0.126 | -0.05, -0.23, -0.11 | 3/3 | 0.00e+00 | large-N |
| E:E | -0.082 | -0.07, -0.12, -0.05 | 3/3 | 1.09e-132 | large-N |
| E:N | -0.066 | -0.05, -0.11, -0.04 | 3/3 | 6.65e-73 | large-N |
| E:D | -0.033 | -0.03, -0.05, -0.02 | 3/3 | 2.37e-19 | large-N |
| A:E | -0.084 | -0.06, -0.19, -0.00 | 2/3 | 0.00e+00 | large-N |
| P:E | -0.081 | -0.12, -0.12, -0.01 | 2/3 | 2.33e-120 | large-N |

: {tbl-colwidths="[10, 16, 30, 10, 16, 18]"}

#### Direction-flip cells (42 of 60 cells: at least one sign change across the 3 timepoints)

36 of the 42 register the change at `p_adj` < 0.05 on both opposite-sign rows. Per `flip-sig-large-N-artifact`, this count is N-driven; rank by `|log2_OR|` range, not by `#sig`. Top 15 by `#sig` descending then max `|log2_OR|` descending; 27 more below the cutoff `(#sig, |log2_OR|) = (3/3, 0.128)` are in the data but not listed.

| cell | log2_OR range | per-tp log2_OR | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A:W | [-0.221, +0.453] | +0.45\*, +0.17\*, -0.22\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| A:P | [-0.123, +0.330] | +0.10\*, +0.33\*, -0.12\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| E:P | [-0.104, +0.211] | +0.20\*, +0.21\*, -0.10\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| A:N | [-0.174, +0.205] | -0.17\*, -0.15\*, +0.20\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| E:M | [-0.200, +0.034] | -0.05\*, -0.20\*, +0.03\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| A:A | [-0.098, +0.193] | +0.04\*, +0.19\*, -0.10\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| A:Y | [-0.191, +0.088] | +0.09\*, -0.06\*, -0.19\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| A:I | [-0.177, +0.049] | -0.18\*, -0.16\*, +0.05\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| A:L | [-0.068, +0.176] | +0.16\*, +0.18\*, -0.07\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| E:W | [-0.176, +0.117] | +0.03\*, -0.18\*, +0.12\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| A:K | [-0.175, +0.047] | -0.02\*, -0.17\*, +0.05\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| E:T | [-0.031, +0.150] | -0.03\*, +0.15\*, -0.02\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| E:G | [-0.133, +0.023] | -0.04\*, -0.13\*, +0.02\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| P:V | [-0.128, +0.086] | +0.02\*, -0.13\*, +0.09\* | 3/3 | large-N |
| P:D | [-0.088, +0.128] | +0.13\*, -0.09\*, -0.03\* | 3/3 | large-N |

: {tbl-colwidths="[14, 22, 30, 14, 20]"}

### Flag glossary

- `large-N` — the row's `p_adj` is below 1e-10 (for cross-timepoint cells, its `min p_adj` is). At whole-transcriptome pooled N Fisher's exact returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations, so `-log10(p_adj)` is inflated; the odds ratio (`log2_OR`) is the primary effect column, and rows should be ranked by `|log2_OR|`, not by p magnitude. A blank flag means the row is FDR-significant but its `p_adj` is at or above 1e-10.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 180 (3 timepoints x 3 sites x 20 AAs)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 159 (88.3%)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 164 (91.1%)
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.0 (underflow; 4 cells at exactly 0.0 to double precision). Smallest non-zero p_adj is 2.21e-289 (day_0 A:D, log2OR=-0.1905).
- Cells with p_adj < 1e-10: 106 of 180
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher's exact has no analytic floor; the relevant discipline here is large-N anti-conservatism (see caveats), not a discreteness floor (per-cell counts are in the thousands).
- Cross-(timepoint, site) BH families and their n_sig at p_adj<0.05:

| timepoint | A | P | E | BWM_total | control_total | ctrl:BWM ratio |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| day_0 | 20/20 | 18/20 | 18/20 | 2,110,322 | 3,364,978 | 1.59 |
| day_5 | 16/20 | 17/20 | 20/20 | 2,014,776 | 2,414,875 | 1.20 |
| day_10 | 15/20 | 17/20 | 18/20 | 1,555,473 | 1,264,927 | 0.81 |

: {tbl-colwidths="[18, 8, 8, 8, 19, 19, 20]"}

- Cross-timepoint (site, aa) cells, all 3 timepoints same OR direction: 18/60 (12 BWM-enriched, 6 BWM-depleted)
- Cross-timepoint (site, aa) cells with at least one sign change: 42/60
- Cells with sign change at p_adj<0.05 on both opposite-sign rows: 36/42 (N-driven; see `flip-sig-large-N-artifact`)

## Biological interpretation

**The near-universal FDR significance is a large-N artifact, not biology (concrete).** 159 of 180 tests clear FDR<0.05, 106 of 180 sit below `p_adj` 1e-10, and 4 cells underflow to exactly 0.0. But the pooled per-timepoint totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (1.3M-3.4M), and at that N Fisher's exact returns a vanishing p for a trivially small composition difference. The honest reading is that essentially every amino acid differs "significantly" between BWM and control at every site and timepoint, which means significance carries no information here and the effect sizes do. `log2_OR` is the column to read.

**Effect sizes are uniformly small, and the largest single-cell divergences cluster at day_5 (concrete).** The biggest `|log2_OR|` in the whole file is day_5 A-site proline (+0.3300, about a 1.26x odds ratio), and the next tier — day_5 E:Y +0.2272, day_5 E:K -0.2260, day_10 A:W -0.2209, day_5 P:W +0.2206 — all sit between 0.20 and 0.33. No cell approaches the +/-0.50 effect guide drawn on the volcano panels. So the BWM-vs-control occupancy difference, while statistically certain, is a small compositional shift, not a wholesale remodelling of which residues occupy the decoding channel.

**The day-5 A-site proline enrichment is the one cell with an obvious mechanistic anchor, but it is a flip cell (exploratory).** A-site proline is the canonical exit-tunnel and peptidyl-transfer slow step (eIF5A/EF-P-dependent rescue), so a BWM enrichment of proline-occupied A sites at day_5 (+0.3300) is at least consistent with a proline-decoding bottleneck being more pronounced in BWM at that timepoint. It is exploratory, not a finding: A:P actually changes sign across the three timepoints (it appears in the direction-flip table with range [-0.123, +0.330]), so a single timepoint's enrichment in an N-saturated file is a lead to test in the codon-resolution sister and in the within-condition Fisher contrasts, not a conclusion.

**A small set of cells holds one direction across all three timepoints and reads like a stable BWM-vs-control compositional offset (exploratory).** 18 of 60 (site, amino acid) cells are direction-concordant. The most consistent enrichments are E-site phenylalanine (+0.110 mean), E-site cysteine (+0.107), A-site glutamine (+0.086), and P-site glutamine (+0.085); the most consistent depletions are E-site lysine (-0.126), A-site glutamate (-0.084), and E-site glutamate (-0.082). These are small but sign-stable offsets, and a difference that persists across the whole time course is more likely a baseline composition feature than a timepoint-specific perturbation. Whether they are BWM-specific or shared by both conditions is exactly what the within-condition binomial answers.

**The 42 direction-flip cells are not 42 biological reversals (concrete).** 36 of the 42 register their sign change at `p_adj` < 0.05 on both opposite-sign rows, which looks dramatic, but at whole-transcriptome N almost any small per-cell sign wobble clears FDR. These should be ranked by the spread of their `log2_OR` (A:W swings [-0.221, +0.453], A:P [-0.123, +0.330], E:P [-0.104, +0.211]), not by how many timepoints reach significance. A flip is only worth biological attention if it reproduces in the codon sister and in the within-condition Fisher contrasts; a flip seen only here is most parsimoniously per-cell noise amplified by the large N.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: significance is near-universal and N-driven, effects are small (max `|log2_OR|` 0.33), and per-(timepoint, site) significant-counts are uniformly high (15/20 to 20/20) with no day_0 power gradient because the two arms are near-balanced (ctrl:BWM 1.59 / 1.20 / 0.81). *Exploratory*: the day_5 A-site proline enrichment (has a mechanistic anchor, but is a flip cell — test in the codon sister); the 18-cell concordant set as a stable BWM-vs-control offset hypothesis (test in `within_condition_binomial`); the largest-spread flip cells (A:W, A:P, E:P) only if they reproduce across designs. *Noise*: the raw p magnitudes and the "36 significant flips" count, both of which are large-N artifacts rather than biological signal.

## Methods

Fisher's exact (two-sided) on a 2x2 contingency `BWM_count` vs `control_count` for each (timepoint, site, amino acid), with BH-FDR within each (timepoint, site) family of 20 amino acids. The effect is reported as `log2_OR` (the log2 of the CSV `odds_ratio` column); `log2_OR` > 0 means BWM-enriched relative to control at that cell, < 0 means BWM-depleted. The p-correction column is `p_adj` (BH per (timepoint, site) family, applied by `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`). The test answers "is the BWM-vs-control amino-acid composition at this site different at this timepoint?". It does not answer "is each condition's per-aa occupancy enriched vs its transcriptomic background" (that is the `within_condition_binomial` family) and does not test "is the BWM-vs-control difference itself shifting across timepoints" (that needs an explicit between-timepoint contrast; the cross-timepoint summary above is descriptive only).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The 2x2 contingencies pool the two biological replicates per (condition, timepoint) before the test, so per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic and p-values run anti-conservative — read `log2_OR` as the effect and do not treat a tiny p as extra certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled totals are whole-transcriptome footprint counts (1.3M-3.4M per timepoint), so Fisher returns vanishing p (4 cells underflow to 0.0; 106/180 below 1e-10) for trivially small deviations — rank by `log2_OR`, never by p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(timepoint,site).** BH is applied within each of the 9 (timepoint, site) families of 20 amino acids, not across the full 180-test grid, so two cells with the same raw p in different (timepoint, site) families can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a cell, not across the grid.
- **imbalanced-N.** The two arms differ in total per timepoint; Fisher handles that correctly, but do not over-read an effect where one cell is small. Here the imbalance is mild (see `n-asymmetry-mild`).
- **n-asymmetry-mild.** Totals are near-balanced (ctrl:BWM 1.59 / 1.20 / 0.81; BWM is the larger arm by day_10), far milder than the stall-sites sister's 4.55x at day_0, and per-(timepoint, site) significant-counts stay uniformly high (15/20 to 20/20) with no day_0 power gradient — so cross-timepoint count differences here are not power-modulated.
- **flip-sig-large-N-artifact.** 36 of the 42 direction-flip cells register the sign change at `p_adj` < 0.05 on both opposite-sign rows; at this N that is power, not 36 biological reversals, so rank flips by `|log2_OR|` range, not by the count of significant timepoints.

### Considered and ruled out

- **OR-direction-anchor.** Proposed as a standalone direction caveat; instead the effect is displayed as `log2_OR` (its sign encodes BWM-enriched vs BWM-depleted), so the convention is stated in Methods and Top hits rather than carried as a separate caveat.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** 0 of 180 rows fall below 100 counts in either arm; at whole-transcriptome N every amino acid is well-counted in both arms, so no rare-AA instability applies (unlike the stall-sites sister, where W/M/C had low-count cells).
- **small-bh-family-discreteness.** Per-cell counts are in the thousands, so the Fisher p-values are effectively continuous and do not cluster at coarse BH levels; the discreteness concern from the small-count stall-sites file does not apply here.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-0 A-site BWM-vs-control amino-acid volcano: log2(odds ratio) on x (-0.50 to +0.50), -log10(FDR) on y; glyphs are amino-acid one-letter codes coloured by class, triangles are FDR<0.05. Largest enrichment at W (tryptophan, ~+0.45, the file's largest single-cell effect); largest depletions at D (aspartate, ~-0.19), I (isoleucine), and N (asparagine); C, L, and P are also enriched.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_A_volcano.png)

![Day-0 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano (same axes and colour key as the other panels). Largest enrichments at G (glycine, ~+0.14) and D (aspartate, ~+0.13); largest depletions at M (methionine, ~-0.13) and E (glutamate, ~-0.12); F enriched, K depleted.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_P_volcano.png)

![Day-0 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: proline (P, ~+0.20) is the largest enrichment and sits highest on the panel; histidine (H, ~-0.11) is the largest depletion; C, F, and L are also enriched and E, N, K depleted.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_0_E_volcano.png)

![Day-5 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: proline (P, ~+0.33, far right) is the largest enrichment in the whole file; alanine (A) and leucine (L) are also enriched; glutamate (E, ~-0.19), lysine (K), and isoleucine (I) are the largest depletions.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_A_volcano.png)

![Day-5 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano: tryptophan (W, ~+0.22) and glutamine (Q, ~+0.20) are the largest enrichments; valine (V, ~-0.13) and glutamate (E, ~-0.12) are the largest depletions.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_P_volcano.png)

![Day-5 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: tyrosine (Y, ~+0.23), proline (P), phenylalanine (F), and alanine (A) cluster as the largest enrichments; lysine (K, ~-0.23, far left) and methionine (M, ~-0.20) are the largest depletions.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_5_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10 A-site BWM-vs-control volcano: asparagine (N, ~+0.20) and glutamine (Q, ~+0.14) are the largest enrichments; tryptophan (W, ~-0.22), tyrosine (Y, ~-0.19), and proline (P, ~-0.12) are the largest depletions.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10 P-site BWM-vs-control volcano (same axes and colour key as the other panels): tryptophan (W, ~+0.14) and leucine (L, ~+0.13) are the largest enrichments; tyrosine (Y, ~-0.12) and asparagine (N, ~-0.08) are the largest depletions.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10 E-site BWM-vs-control volcano: cysteine (C, ~+0.16, far right) is the largest enrichment and tryptophan (W, ~+0.12) is also enriched; lysine (K, ~-0.11, top left) and proline (P, ~-0.10) are the largest depletions.](../plots/per_timepoint_fisher/individual/day_10_E_volcano.png)
